definitions.text: ISO 639-2 ISO 639-5 language code for Austronesian languages.
A visual representation of an area, whether real or imaginary, showing the relative positions of places and other features.
examples: a map of Australia, a map of Lilliput
He bought a map for the tour of Edinburgh Castle.

definitions.text: A graphical or logical representation of any structure or system, showing the positions of or relationships between its components.
examples: a map of the Earth's magnetic field

definitions.text: To represent by means of a map.
examples: This large atlas maps the whole world in very great detail.
Figure 3 maps the pressure distribution within the human circulatory system.

definitions.text: To create a map of; to examine or survey in order to gather information for a map.
examples: The team is mapping the route of the new railway line.
The space probe is mapping the Earth's gravitational field.

definitions.text: To have a direct relationship; to correspond.
examples: This doesn't map to my understanding of how things should work.
